eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects caution around reaching 1550 Elo on the Chatbot Arena leaderboard in 2026, with "None" at 56% implied probability due to the current top score hovering around 1503—led by Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7-thinking—still roughly 50 points short amid slowing scaling progress and compute constraints. Anthropic commands 30.5% as the frontrunner after their April 16 release of Claude Opus 4.7, whose "thinking" variants rapidly dominated the top five spots via superior human-preference battles, outpacing Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro preview (1492 Elo, 11.5%) and OpenAI's GPT-5.5-high (11%). xAI's Grok trails further, contributing to low odds. Key catalysts ahead include OpenAI's next frontier model and regulatory hurdles on AI training, with leaderboard volatility from ongoing battles underscoring uncertainty.

markets.question: Will "Pinocchio" be said on ICEMAN?
markets.description:
Drake is expected to release his new album "ICEMAN" in 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone on the specified transcript for any track on the officially released full "ICEMAN" album. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Officially released means that Iceman is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date. Any Drake album officially confirmed to be the Iceman project will count, regardless of potential name changes.

Samples or clips of audio where people are speaking or singing which are included in the album will qualify toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript. AI-generated audio will count toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript.

In the case a term is censored on a relevant transcript, audio may be consulted to verify that a qualifying mention occurred.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the initial release of the full album on a qualifying streaming platform. Releases consisting only of individual songs or remixes released later will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be the audio transcripts for this album provided by the listed streaming platforms in the following order of priority: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, Amazon Music, Tidal (e.g., Spotify will be used unless it does not provide a necessary transcript, then Apple Music will be used, etc.).

If no such album is released in full by by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to based on what has been released up to that point. If the album is not released on any of the listed platforms by the specified end date, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Drake's ninth studio album *Iceman*, set for release tomorrow on May 15 via OVO Sound and Republic Records, drives intense trader focus on lyrical mentions amid a viral Toronto ice sculpture scavenger hunt and icy website redesign of his Embassy estate. Consensus tilts toward introspective themes like fatherhood ("Daddy") and pandemic reflections ("Covid"), reflecting Drake's post-beef pivot after Kendrick Lamar's "Not Like Us," with low odds for direct disses despite lingering fan speculation. No leaks have surfaced, heightening uncertainty; resolution hinges on Spotify transcripts post-drop, alongside bets on first-week sales and Billboard 200 debut. Drake's ongoing streaming dominance—top global artist yesterday—bolsters frontrunner momentum for chart-topping performance.

markets.question: Benedict Wong as Wong?
markets.description:
Avengers: Doomsday is a Marvel film scheduled to release on December, 18, 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed actor playing the listed character appears in Avengers: Doomsday. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

This market will resolve upon announcement of casting, regardless of if the film is delayed, cancelled, or otherwise fails to be released. If no casting is announced, this market will resolve upon footage of the domestic theatrical cut of the film. If multiple people are cast for the same role, the listed individual being among the cast list for the relevant character will be sufficient to qualify this market towards a "Yes" resolution.

If no casting is announced and Avengers: Doomsday is definitively cancelled or fails to be released by June 30, 2027, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve based on official announcements from Marvel or Warner Bros., the domestic theatrical cut of Avengers: Doomsday, or a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Marvel Studios' Avengers: Doomsday has solidified trader consensus around a sprawling ensemble through official announcements and CinemaCon 2026 footage, confirming Robert Downey Jr. as the multiversal Doctor Doom clashing with Thor (Chris Hemsworth), new Captain America (Anthony Mackie), Steve Rogers (Chris Evans wielding Mjolnir), Fantastic Four leads like Pedro Pascal's Reed Richards and Vanessa Kirby's Sue Storm, plus X-Men icons including Patrick Stewart's Professor X, Ian McKellen's Magneto, and Alan Cumming's Nightcrawler. markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

events.title: Will EUR/USD hit __ in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices EUR/USD hitting key 2026 levels amid stark monetary policy divergence between the European Central Bank and Federal Reserve. The ECB's March 19 decision to hold key rates steady reflects a hawkish pivot, with upgraded inflation forecasts to 2.6% driven by geopolitical tensions including the Iran conflict, prompting surveys for two rate hikes this year. Meanwhile, the Fed targets federal funds at 3.5-3.75% post-April minutes, with markets pricing just one cut amid resilient U.S. labor data. Current spot at 1.174 reflects USD strength, with forecasts clustering around 1.13-1.27 range. Watch June 16-17 FOMC and July ECB meetings, plus Eurozone CPI and U.S. nonfarm payrolls, for catalysts shifting implied probabilities.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Lagos be 31°C on May 14?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Murtala Muhammad International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 14 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Murtala Muhammad International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ng/lagos/DNMM.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight uncertainty in forecast models for Lagos's highest temperature on May 14, with 33°C at 33.5% edging 32°C at 29%, driven by NiMet's latest outlook predicting partly sunny skies interspersed with isolated thunderstorms typical of May's wet season onset. Recent observations show May 13 highs nearing 33°C amid fading harmattan influences and high humidity suppressing nighttime cooling, while historical May averages hover at 31–32°C per NOAA climatology. Key differentiators include cloud cover variability—thicker convective clouds could cap peaks at 32°C, whereas clearer intervals might push to 33°C—against a backdrop of urban heat island effects in Lagos. Updated NiMet bulletins expected later today may refine this before resolution based on official airport measurements.

markets.question: Joselu Perez: Anytime Goalscorer
markets.description: In the upcoming La Liga game between Getafe CF and RCD Mallorca, scheduled for May 13, 2026 at 3:30 PM ET: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Joselu Perez is credited with a goal in the official box score within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". Own goals do not count. If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "50-50". If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not play, the market will resolve "No". The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Getafe CF vs. RCD Mallorca - Player Props
events.description: Goal scorers for the La Liga game between Getafe CF and RCD Mallorca, scheduled for May 13, 2026 at 3:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mallorca enters the Matchday 36 clash at Getafe's Coliseum Alfonso Pérez hampered by key absences, including suspended midfielder Samú Costa, muscle-injured wingback Johan Mojica, and doubtful defender Antonio Raíllo recovering from ankle surgery, weakening their midfield control and defensive structure as they sit 15th in relegation peril with 39 points. Getafe, 7th with 45 points chasing Europe, boasts a healthier squad despite minor concerns for Mauro Arambarri (team-leading 6 goals) and Martín Satriano (knee), relying on Luis Milla's 9 assists in their compact 5-3-2 setup amid poor recent scoring (just 1 goal in last 5 league games). Mallorca's Vedat Muriqi (21 goals, Pichichi contender) remains a props focal point after scoring in their 1-1 Villarreal draw, but Getafe's home defense and low-scoring H2H trends (three 1-goal margins in last five) temper expectations in this tight late-season encounter.

markets.question: Will Orinats Yerkir win the most seats in the 2026 Armenian National Assembly election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Armenia on June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Armenian National Assembly in this election.

If voting in the Armenian parliamentary election does not occur by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition which received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Armenian National Assembly. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Armenian government, specifically the Armenian Central Election Commission (https://www.elections.am/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Civil Contract's commanding trader consensus at 93.5% implied probability stems from recent EVN Report/ArmES polling (April-May) showing the incumbent party at 32.5% support—well ahead of Strong Armenia's 10.1% and other fragmented opposition alliances—amid 39.5% undecided voters trending toward the ruling bloc in prior waves. Armenia's closed-list proportional representation system favors the party securing the highest nationwide vote share as winner, reinforced by Civil Contract's historical dominance (53.9% in 2021 snap election) and opposition disunity across 19 competing forces. The official campaign period opened last Friday, highlighting geopolitical tensions from Prime Minister Pashinyan's Western pivot post-Nagorno-Karabakh. Scenarios challenging this include late scandals, opposition consolidation, or depressed turnout favoring protesters, though barriers remain high ahead of the June 7 vote.

markets.question: Will Solana dip to $20 by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to “Yes” if any Binance 1-minute candle for Solana (SOL/USDT) between November 24, 2025, 14:30 and December 31, 2026, 23:59 in the ET timezone has a final “Low” price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT “Low” prices available at:
https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT with the chart settings on “1m” (one-minute candles) selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance SOL/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

markets.question: Will Toluca win Liga MX?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club is determined as the champion of the Liga MX. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed participant to be named as the champion of Liga MX per the rules of Liga MX (e.g., participant is eliminated), the corresponding market will resolve to "No".

If multiple teams are declared winners, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

If this event is cancelled, postponed after June 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or a champion has not been declared within this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the Liga MX; however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Toluca at 44.9% implied probability to claim the Liga MX Clausura 2026 title, propelled by their regular-season dominance atop the table with 37 points, a +25 goal difference, and league-leading 43 goals scored amid recent unbeaten streak (W-D-D-D-W). Cruz Azul (28.5%) and Pumas UNAM (28.0%) advanced through quarterfinals past Atlas (4-2 aggregate) and América (aggregate after 3-3 second leg), leveraging solid home form and liguilla experience as third- and first-seed respectively. Guadalajara (25.0%) staged an epic remontada to oust Tigres UANL in quarterfinals, boosting momentum into semis against Cruz Azul, while Pachuca (21.6%) swept Toluca 3-0 aggregate but now faces top-seed Pumas. Semifinals loom May 13-14 amid tight matchups and home advantages in second legs.

markets.question: Counter-Strike: 5star vs FlyQuest (BO3) - Asian Champions League Group A
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Lower bracket quarterfinal 1 match between 5star and FlyQuest in the Asian Champions League Group A, initially scheduled for May 12 at 11:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "5star" if 5star win the match against FlyQuest.

This market will resolve to "FlyQuest" if FlyQuest win the match against 5star.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: FlyQuest holds a clear edge over 5star in Group A lower bracket round 2 of the Hero Esports Asian Champions League 2026 BO3, reflecting trader consensus on their superior roster depth and recent momentum. The Australian squad, ranked #57 on HLTV with veterans like ex-Liquid AWPer jks and rifler INS, rebounded from a 1-2 upper quarterfinal loss to JiJieHao via a commanding 2-0 win over NEXVOID yesterday on Anubis and Dust II. 5star (#97), fresh off a gritty 2-1 upset of Rare Atom but a 0-2 rout by TYLOO, shows inconsistency against top teams in the current CS2 meta favoring structured T-sides. No head-to-head history; FlyQuest's map pool strengths (88.9% Anubis win rate) and experience drive favoritism in this offline Shanghai double-elim clash.

markets.question: Will G2 Esports qualify to MSI 2026?
markets.description:
The MSI 2026 (Mid-Season Invitational) is scheduled to take place from June 26 to July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for MSI 2026.

If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to MSI 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official LoL Esports rules.

If MSI 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of MSI 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the LoL Esports website (https://lolesports.com). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Mid-Season_Invitational/2026) may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Secret Whales clinched LCP's second MSI 2026 spot via championship points on May 9, the first qualification amid their undefeated Split 2 run, while playoffs across major regions remain wide open. LPL squads hold a Bracket Stage bye from First Stand triumph, boosting their frontrunners like LGD Gaming and LNG Esports (both 3-1 in Split 2). LEC playoffs loom after regular season finale, led by Team Vitality (8-1) and Karmine Corp (7-2) over G2 and Movistar KOI. LCS features undefeated Cloud9 and Team Liquid with one week left before playoffs; LCK's Hanwha Life Esports dominates Split 2 at 11-1 ahead of Road to MSI; CBLOL's RED Canids (6-1) eyes the lone slot. Regional grand finals wrap by mid-June.

eventMetadata.context_description: In European Pro League Regular Season 5 Group C's double-elimination stage, SINQU Esports (1-1 record, 6-26 map score) meets KUUSAMO.gg (0-2, 9-26) in a pivotal BO3 elimination match for playoff advancement. SINQU dropped 0-2 to ALGO Esports on May 7, struggling on Anubis and Mirage after a single map push on Ancient, amid a roster-stable but winless streak over the past three months across ESL Challenger and Digital Crusade events. KUUSAMO.gg fell 0-2 to BIG Academy the same day on Inferno and Dust2, hampered by a recently revamped lineup (players joined 1-2 months ago) posting a 33% win rate lately. No CS2 head-to-head; trader consensus reflects SINQU's edge in group positioning despite mutual tier-3 form woes.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ella Langley's "Choosin' Texas" continues its dominant run atop the Billboard Hot 100, marking nine non-consecutive weeks at number one as of the May 16 chart and securing her as a first-time chart-topper, while her new collab with Morgan Wallen debuts at #7. Bruno Mars' "I Just Might" climbs to #2, and Olivia Dean's "Man I Need" surges to #3 amid strong streaming gains. Olivia Rodrigo recently snagged a brief #1 with "Drop Dead" in early May, adding to 2026's eight unique number-one artists including Taylor Swift, BTS, and Bad Bunny. Drake's "Iceman" album drops May 15, with trader forecasts on platforms like Kalshi predicting multi-week Billboard 200 dominance and potential Hot 100 sweep, setting up fierce competition for the next charts through June releases from aespa and Bleachers.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Ukraine coup attempt by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a widely reported coup attempt in Ukraine at any point between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

A coup attempt is defined as a coordinated and deliberate effort by military, security forces, or other state actors (or factions thereof) to overthrow or unlawfully seize control of the Ukrainian government or its leadership. Revolutionary actions by non-state forces, isolated protests, or general unrest will not alone qualify.

Claims by the Ukrainian government that a coup attempt was foiled—such as through arrests or disruption of a planned operation—will only count toward a "Yes" resolution if credible independent sources widely report and explicitly characterize the event as a coup attempt. Foiled plots or arrests that occur without any attempted execution of the coup will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects overwhelming stability in Ukraine's government, with no credible reports of coup plots or military disloyalty emerging in the past 30 days amid the ongoing Russian invasion. President Zelenskyy maintains firm control, bolstered by unified command structures and Western support, despite isolated anti-mobilization protests in cities like Odessa that have not escalated to systemic threats. Recent European intelligence has instead highlighted Kremlin paranoia over potential internal coups against Putin, including fears of elite-driven assassinations, diverting attention from Kyiv. Absent verifiable catalysts like high-level arrests, defections, or no-confidence signals before June 30, traders price negligible risk of a successful challenge to Zelenskyy's authority.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round of 16 match between KOLESIE and TDK in the CCT Europe Series 1 Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 13 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if KOLESIE and TDK play 3 or more maps in this series.

If fewer than 3 maps are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round of 16 match between KOLESIE and TDK in the CCT Europe Series 1 Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 13 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "KOLESIE" if KOLESIE win the match against TDK.

This market will resolve to "TDK" if TDK win the match against KOLESIE.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: TDK carries momentum into the CCT Europe Series 1 playoffs Round of 16 BO3 against KOLESIE after a 3-1 group stage run, featuring 2-0 shutouts over Famalicão and GenOne plus a 2-1 win against Lavked on May 10, offsetting an opening loss to INOX Division. KOLESIE, a direct playoff invite at HLTV #71 (ahead of TDK's #86), shows mixed form with recent losses to Walczaki (1-2, April 29) and SINNERS (0-2, April 26), though bolstered by April roster additions fr3nd and ex1st. No head-to-head history exists; TDK excels on Overpass (81% win rate) and Dust2 (69%), contrasting KOLESIE's strengths on Ancient (68%) and Nuke (73%), shaping trader consensus on map vetoes and stylistic edges.

markets.question: Will the next governing coalition of New Zealand include National + Labour?
markets.description:
A general election is scheduled to be held in New Zealand on November 7, 2026.

This market will resolve to the first ruling coalition of parties that forms after the next New Zealand general election.

A party will only be considered part of the ruling coalition if it participates in the governing coalition and provides at least one Cabinet minister. Parties that merely support the government from outside Cabinet, including through confidence-and-supply agreements or similar arrangements, without holding a Cabinet post will not qualify.

The following parties will be considered for this market: ACT New Zealand (ACT), Labour Party (Labour), Te Pāti Māori (Maori), National Party (National), Green Party (Green), and New Zealand First Party (NZF). All other parties will not be considered.

Resolution will be based on the listed option that most completely matches the parties included in the coalition. If the coalition formed after the election includes all of the parties listed in a market option, along with any other parties, that option will resolve to “Yes” unless there exists another market option that more completely covers the coalition. A listed option will not resolve to “Yes” if any of its listed parties are not included in the coalition.

For example:

- If the governing coalition includes Labour + Green + ACT, but no option explicitly lists those three together, the market will resolve to “Labour + Green” if that option is listed and no listed option more completely matches the coalition.

- If the governing coalition includes National + ACT + NZF, the option “National + ACT + NZF” will resolve to “Yes”, while “National + ACT” will resolve to “No”.

In the event that the ruling coalition includes all parties for multiple market options, and each option contains an equal number of coalition parties, the tie will be resolved in favor of the option whose listed parties together hold the greater number of seats in the newly elected House of Representatives. If this presents another tie, this market will resolve in favor of the listed option whose parties received the greatest number of valid party votes.

If any party forms a single-party majority government, the listed option for that party will be considered the governing coalition.

If the governing coalition does not include all parties in any listed option, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market may resolve once the first government is officially confirmed following the appointment of the Prime Minister and ministers by the Governor-General after the 2026 New Zealand general election, with ministers appointed under non-caretaker circumstances.

If the next New Zealand ruling coalition after the election is not confirmed by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve based on official information from the government of New Zealand.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Seattle be between 58-59°F on May 15?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 15 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Seattle-Tacoma International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/wa/seatac/KSEA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors 57°F or below at 51% implied probability for Seattle's highest temperature on May 15, driven by the latest National Weather Service guidance and GFS/ECMWF model ensembles projecting highs near 57°F at Seattle-Tacoma International Airport under persistent marine stratus clouds and a 50-60% chance of showers. A weakening upper-level ridge is yielding to an approaching trough, ushering cooler Pacific air and suppressing daytime heating after a warmer early-May pattern. Recent 00z model runs yesterday reinforced this cooldown, with low-level onshore flow enhancing cloud cover. While inherent forecast uncertainty persists—drier scenarios could nudge highs to 58-59°F—new NWS updates and 12z models today may refine probabilities ahead of resolution.

markets.question: Will Z.ai have the #3 AI model at the end of May 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the third-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies third place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus pins United Kingdom and Austria as virtual dead heats for Eurovision 2026 last place in Vienna's Grand Final, driven by their entries' tepid previews and rehearsals amid powerhouse qualifiers like Moldova and Sweden from Semi-Final 1. The UK's "Eins, Zwei, Drei" by Look Mum No Computer, a quirky electronic rant on work woes (3.3-star fan rating), fuels apathy and zero-televote fears, extending the BBC's dismal streak. Host Austria's "Tanzschein" by Cosmó, a niche German club metaphor (2.8 stars), lacks broad appeal despite home advantage. Belgium's bland "Dancing on the Ice" by Essyla crashed out 11th in Semi 1, capping its threat; Germany and Armenia face steeper odds in a crowded field. Semi-Final 2 results Thursday could reshape dynamics before Saturday's jury-televote split.

An announced agreement between the listed company and an acquiring entity will qualify for a “Yes” resolution, regardless of whether the acquisition is ultimately completed.

markets.question: Will Arsenal finish in 2nd place in the 2025-26 English Premier League?
markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club finishes 2nd in the final standings of the 2025–26 English Premier League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to finish 2nd in the 2025-26 EPL season (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: English Premier League – 2nd Place
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will finish in 2nd place in the 2025–26 English Premier League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal's commanding position atop the Premier League table with 79 points from 36 games and a +42 goal difference has solidified trader consensus on Manchester City finishing second at 86.5% implied probability, as the Citizens trail by five points but hold a crucial game in hand after 35 matches. City's recent 3-0 win over Brentford narrowed the gap temporarily, yet Arsenal's run of three straight victories—including against West Ham—and stronger remaining fixtures like hosting Burnley position them as title frontrunners. A City second-place lockout could unravel if they perfect their final three games (starting with Crystal Palace at home) while Arsenal drops points in their last two, allowing an overtake amid the tight title race; distant chasers like Manchester United (65 points) pose no realistic threat.

markets.question: Will Movimento Democrático Brasileiro (MDB) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

Recent state polls, such as Paraná Pesquisas in Paraná and DataTrends in Pernambuco, alongside PL pre-candidate launches by figures like André do Prado in São Paulo and Eder Mauro in Pará, underscore competitive positioning across conservative strongholds, bolstered by right-wing unity and Bolsonaro family involvement. MDB holds second at 12% on incumbency advantages in centrist states, while PODEMOS, UNIÃO, and PT lag amid fragmented fields; primaries and debates ahead could influence outcomes in closely contested races.

eventMetadata.context_description: OpenAI's GPT-5.5, released April 23, 2026, currently holds frontier-leading scores on the FrontierMath benchmark—around 52% on Tiers 1-3 and 35-40% on the grueling Tier 4 research-level math problems—with GPT-5.4 slightly ahead overall at 47.6% per third-party leaderboards. Trader sentiment hinges on yesterday's Epoch AI announcement of fatal errors in roughly one-third of Tiers 1-4 problems, flagged by GPT-5.5 itself, triggering a human review that could boost revised scores and reshape capability claims. Google DeepMind's May 8 multi-agent model hit 47.9% on Tier 4, intensifying competition, while OpenAI's rapid iteration cadence leaves room for a GPT-5.6 preview before June 30 resolution.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the CO-01 House seat?

This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the CO-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Perry Johnson at 52% to win Michigan's Republican gubernatorial primary on August 4, ahead of U.S. Rep. John James at 40%, driven by Johnson's self-funded advertising blitz and pledges for government efficiency, including eliminating the state income tax—gains that propelled his prediction market odds from near parity in early April to a clear lead by early May. Recent Emerson (April) and Detroit Regional Chamber polls showed a tight race or slight James edge among likely primary voters, bolstered by his congressional profile, with former AG Mike Cox at 10%. However, dueling signature challenges alleging fraud, filed May 8 by each camp against the other, echo Johnson's 2022 disqualification and heighten ballot access risks ahead of Bureau of Elections review.

markets.question: Will Citigroup fail by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed bank fails between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

For the purposes of this market, the listed bank will be considered to have “failed” if, within the listed date range, any of the following occurs under the bank’s applicable legal or regulatory framework:

- The listed bank’s primary banking regulator formally declares the institution insolvent or non-viable, or withdraws or revokes the bank’s license or authorization, and such determination initiates or directly results in resolution, liquidation, wind-down, or transfer actions.
- The listed bank enters a court-ordered liquidation, statutory resolution regime, or regulator-mandated wind-down, including the use of resolution tools such as bail-ins, forced asset transfers, or the establishment of a bridge bank.
- A government or resolution authority intervenes in a manner that wipes out or subordinates existing equity of the listed bank and transfers effective control of the bank to the state or a designated resolution authority, with continued operations dependent on official intervention.
- The listed bank publicly defaults on a payment obligation, including derivatives margin, repo, or physical commodity delivery, and such default is formally acknowledged by the bank’s primary regulator or resolution authority and directly results in the initiation of resolution, liquidation, license withdrawal, or regulator-mandated transfer of the bank.
- The listed bank is subject to a compulsory merger, acquisition, or transfer of all or substantially all of its assets and liabilities ordered or directed by its primary banking regulator or resolution authority due to the bank’s financial condition or to prevent failure, regardless of whether a formal insolvency declaration or immediate equity wipeout is publicly announced at the time of transfer.

If there is a potential failure of the listed bank within this market’s date range and a qualifying regulatory or court action has occurred but has not yet been fully published by the relevant authority, this market may remain open  to allow for confirmation. If no qualifying failure is confirmed by that date, this market will resolve to “No.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements, filings, or actions by the listed bank’s primary banking regulator or resolution authority; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders reflect low failure risk for listed major banks like BNY Mellon and KeyBank by June 30 through implied probabilities under 10%, driven by strong capital ratios and deposit betas below 30% amid stabilizing Q1 credit metrics. Sector unrealized losses stand at $306 billion on held-to-maturity securities, but majors have reduced commercial real estate (CRE) exposure ahead of a $875 billion maturity wall this year. Recent small-bank failures—Metropolitan Capital Bank in late January and Community Bank & Trust-West Georgia on May 1—highlight idiosyncratic CRE and liquidity strains without contagion to systemically important institutions. Key catalysts include the June 11-12 FOMC meeting, where the federal funds rate holds at 3.5%-3.75%, and CCAR stress tests.

markets.question: Will Prosperous Armenia win the most seats in the 2026 Armenian National Assembly election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Armenia on June 7, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Armenian National Assembly in this election.

If voting in the Armenian parliamentary election does not occur by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition which received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Armenian National Assembly. 

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Armenian government, specifically the Armenian Central Election Commission (https://www.elections.am/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Civil Contract's commanding trader consensus at 93.5% implied probability stems from recent EVN Report/ArmES polling (April-May) showing the incumbent party at 32.5% support—well ahead of Strong Armenia's 10.1% and other fragmented opposition alliances—amid 39.5% undecided voters trending toward the ruling bloc in prior waves. Armenia's closed-list proportional representation system favors the party securing the highest nationwide vote share as winner, reinforced by Civil Contract's historical dominance (53.9% in 2021 snap election) and opposition disunity across 19 competing forces. The official campaign period opened last Friday, highlighting geopolitical tensions from Prime Minister Pashinyan's Western pivot post-Nagorno-Karabakh. Scenarios challenging this include late scandals, opposition consolidation, or depressed turnout favoring protesters, though barriers remain high ahead of the June 7 vote.

markets.question: New York Red Bulls vs. Columbus Crew: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming MLS game between New York Red Bulls and Columbus Crew, scheduled for May 13 at 7:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both New York Red Bulls and Columbus Crew each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on mlssoccer.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk and Sam Altman settle?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the claims between Elon Musk and Sam Altman in Musk v. Altman et al., Case No. 4:24-cv-04722-YGR (U.S. District Court, Northern District of California), are settled by 11:59 PM ET on December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A settlement refers to an agreement between Elon Musk and Sam Altman that resolves all claims between them in this case, whether through a formal settlement agreement, stipulated dismissal, or other court-recognized resolution indicating that the claims between these two parties have been resolved. Announcements of such a settlement will qualify, regardless of whether final paperwork is completed or court approval occurs after the deadline.

Settlements involving other parties (e.g., OpenAI or any additional defendants) will not count unless they also resolve all claims between Elon Musk and Sam Altman.

If all claims between Elon Musk and Sam Altman are resolved through a final court judgment (e.g., trial verdict or dispositive ruling), or through a unilateral dismissal of claims not constituting a settlement, before any qualifying settlement occurs, this market will immediately resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the U.S. court system (including filings, orders, or docket updates). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects an 78.5% implied probability that Elon Musk and Sam Altman will not settle their high-stakes lawsuit over OpenAI's shift from nonprofit to for-profit structure, driven by Altman's testimony on May 12 in Oakland federal court. Altman denied betraying the original mission, countering that Musk sought up to 90% equity and total control, including passing it to his children, while defending the restructure as essential for scaling frontier AI amid capital demands from Microsoft. Musk's failed pre-trial settlement overture two days before proceedings began, combined with his earlier testimony labeling himself a "fool" for co-founding OpenAI, underscores irreconcilable visions for AI governance and competitive positioning between xAI and OpenAI. With witnesses like Ilya Sutskever and Satya Nadella pending and potential jury deliberations next week, traders see scant path to resolution short of verdict.

The UK's "Eins, Zwei, Drei" by Look Mum No Computer, a quirky electronic rant on work woes (3.3-star fan rating), fuels apathy and zero-televote fears, extending the BBC's dismal streak. Host Austria's "Tanzschein" by Cosmó, a niche German club metaphor (2.8 stars), lacks broad appeal despite home advantage. Belgium's bland "Dancing on the Ice" by Essyla crashed out 11th in Semi 1, capping its threat; Germany and Armenia face steeper odds in a crowded field. Semi-Final 2 results Thursday could reshape dynamics before Saturday's jury-televote split.

markets.question: Will Starmer say "Liz" or "Truss" during the next Prime Minister's Questions event?
markets.description:
Keir Starmer is scheduled to participate in the Prime Minister's Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber in the Palace of Westminster on every Wednesday. (https://www.parliament.uk/visiting/visiting-and-tours/watch-committees-and-debates/prime-ministers-questions/).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Keir Starmer says the listed term during the next Prime Minister's Question Time event he participates in. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no such Prime Minister's Question Time event happens by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If Starmer ceases to be Prime Minister for any length of time by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is explicitly about scheduled events featuring Keir Starmer titled "Prime Minister's Question Time" (https://www.parallelparliament.co.uk/mp/keir-starmer/dept/cabinet-office/calendar). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-16 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-16 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Cordoba: Eduardo Ribeiro vs Murkel Dellien
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Eduardo Ribeiro and Murkel Dellien in the Cordoba, originally scheduled for May 13, 2026 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Eduardo Ribeiro' if Eduardo Ribeiro advances against Murkel Dellien.

This market will resolve to 'Murkel Dellien' if Murkel Dellien advances against Eduardo Ribeiro.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Jerome Powell depart as Fed Chair between June 20 and June 26?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed date range during which Jerome Powell vacates his role as Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors.

Vacating his role refers to Powell actually ceasing to hold the position of Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors. Announcements of Powell's resignation or firing will not alone qualify.

The scheduled end of Powell’s term as Chair will not alone qualify. If Powell continues to serve as Chair on a temporary basis (e.g. until the confirmation of his successor), he will not be considered to have vacated his role as Chair.

This market is restricted to Powell’s status as Chair of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors. Powell’s status as a regular member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not affect this market’s resolution.

All dates refer to the Eastern Time zone (ET).

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Jerome Powell and the Federal Reserve; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The Senate's 51-45 confirmation of President Trump's nominee Kevin Warsh to a Fed governor seat yesterday further solidifies the transition timeline, reflecting historical patterns where chairs exit at term end absent reappointment. Scenarios challenging this include delays in Warsh's chair confirmation vote, an abrupt early resignation, or legal hurdles extending Powell's tenure, though his explicit statements reduce such risks.

markets.question: Will CZ post 0-19 posts from May 15 to May 22, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times CZ (@cz_binance), posts on X between May 15, 12:00 PM ET and May 22, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will the UK designate the IRGC a terrorist organization by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United Kingdom government formally designates the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organization by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

This includes designation as a proscribed terrorist group or organisation according to the UK Home Office, as well as the use of Royal Prerogative Powers or Orders in Council, legislation signed into law, or the creation of a new national designation that formally designates the IRGC as a terrorist organization.

Statements by government officials, including the Prime Minister, Home Office, or National Crime Service, will not count unless accompanied by a formal designation.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting or official information from the UK Government.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a 93.9% implied probability against the UK designating Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organisation by June 30, driven by procedural hurdles in proscribing state-backed entities under the Terrorism Act 2000. Prime Minister Keir Starmer pledged new legislation in April 2026 to enable such bans following the EU's February designation of the IRGC, but the bill faces delayed introduction—potentially via today's King's Speech—with parliamentary stages unlikely to conclude before the deadline. Ongoing sanctions target IRGC activities amid national security threats, yet cross-party calls and Early Day Motions have yielded no action, underscoring slow legislative timelines as the dominant factor. Late-breaking diplomatic shifts or expedited votes remain possible but improbable.

eventMetadata.context_description: Paul Skenes leads trader consensus for the 2026 NL Cy Young at 42.5% implied probability, driven by his elite early-season dominance with a 2.20 ERA, MLB-best 0.67 WHIP, and 52 strikeouts over 45 innings in nine starts for the Pirates, including a sparkling 0.71 WHIP through recent outings as of May 12. Cristopher Sanchez has surged to 10.5% with a 2.11 ERA and NL-high-tying 67 strikeouts, bolstered by a seven-strikeout quality start against Colorado on May 10. Shohei Ohtani's return to pitching fuels his 15.8% share, posting a 0.97 ERA early despite allowing his first homers eight days ago, while Yoshinobu Yamamoto (9.5%) and Chris Sale (7.5%) benefit from Dodgers and Braves rotation stability amid low innings totals league-wide. Markets reflect wisdom of crowds on these pitchers' peripherals amid healthy workloads.

markets.question: Will Josh Elliott win the 2026 Connecticut Governor Democratic primary election?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for Governor of Connecticut, scheduled to take place on August 11, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Connecticut Gubernatorial Democratic Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Connecticut Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Governor Ned Lamont's commanding 94.5% trader consensus in the Connecticut Democratic gubernatorial primary stems from his strong 63% approval ratings, incumbency advantage, and dominant polling leads over challenger State Rep. Josh Elliott, including a 57%-13% margin among likely primary voters in February. Elliott's progressive, long-shot bid from the left has gained modest traction since April but lacks momentum to overcome Lamont's fundraising and party support. Ahead of the May 16 Democratic convention—where Elliott needs 15% delegate backing to trigger the August 11 primary—traders see scant path for upset absent a major scandal, endorsement shift, or late-breaking controversy eroding Lamont's lead.

markets.question: Will Renato Moicano be the UFC Lightweight Champion on December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the official UFC Lightweight division champion on December 31, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET.

Only official UFC division champions will count. Interim champions will not count.

If the relevant belt is vacant at this market’s check time, or there is otherwise no champion in the specified division, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/athletes).

events.title: Who will be UFC Lightweight champion at the end of 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the official UFC Lightweight division champion on December 31, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET.

Only official UFC division champions will count. Interim champions will not count.

If the relevant belt is vacant at this market’s check time, or there is otherwise no champion in the specified division, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/athletes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Ilia Topuria's commanding first-round knockout of Charles Oliveira at UFC 317 late last year propelled him to undisputed lightweight champion status after Islam Makhachev vacated for welterweight, fueling trader consensus at 77% implied probability for him to hold the belt through 2026. His brief early-year hiatus due to personal matters ended without setbacks, positioning him strongly for the June unification against interim champ Justin Gaethje (11.8%), who earned his strap via unanimous decision over Paddy Pimblett but faces Topuria's superior knockout power and undefeated record. Arman Tsarukyan (13%) emerges as the top post-unification contender after recent rankings climbs and stylistic edges over peers, while Oliveira's pedigree lingers at 10.4% despite the title loss. The division's depth tempers underdogs like Benoît Saint Denis (6.2%), with no major injuries altering paths.

markets.question: Will New England Patriots win the 2026 AFC East?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that is declared as the AFC East division champion for the 2026 NFL regular season.

If multiple teams tie atop the division, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the NFL.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors the Buffalo Bills at 58% implied probability to win the AFC East, driven by their aggressive 2026 offseason overhaul after firing Sean McDermott and hiring Joe Brady as head coach, highlighted by trades for WR D.J. Moore and DE Bradley Chubb plus recent signing of LB Mike Danna from Kansas City, fortifying Josh Allen's elite quarterback play and defensive front around a defense-heavy draft class. Reigning champions New England Patriots sit close at 36%, buoyed by their 14-3 2025 campaign, deep playoff run to Super Bowl LX under Mike Vrabel, and Drake Maye's sophomore momentum despite a middling draft grade. New York Jets languish at 7% amid $48 million dead cap from parting with Aaron Rodgers and Justin Fields, leaving QB uncertainty; Miami Dolphins trail at 6% navigating new HC Jeff Hafley and an unsettled quarterback room post-Tua Tagovailoa questions.

markets.question: Will Zendaya and Tom Holland get married by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Zendaya and Tom Holland's marriage takes place by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only announcements made within this market's timeframe will qualify. No announcements made after December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET will be considered, even if the marriage itself is announced to have taken place within the market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from Zendaya and Tom Holland, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket gives a 65% implied probability to Zendaya and Tom Holland tying the knot by year-end, driven primarily by stylist Law Roach's March 1, 2026, red-carpet claim at the Actor Awards that "the wedding has already happened—you missed it," which he affirmed as "very true." This insider remark, reported across major outlets, intensified speculation following their confirmed engagement debut at the 2025 Golden Globes and sightings of a possible wedding band. However, the couple's deliberate privacy—evident in Zendaya's April podcast remarks prioritizing personal boundaries amid parasocial fan investment—prevents overwhelming consensus, leaving room for doubt absent an official statement. Public appearances together sustain momentum, with any direct confirmation as the key near-term catalyst.

markets.question: Will Turkiye win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France and Spain lead trader consensus at 17.8% and 16.4% implied probabilities for the 2026 FIFA World Cup winner, bunched tightly amid a wave of late-season injuries hitting key stars like Kylian Mbappé's hamstring sidelining for France, Lamine Yamal's recovery timeline for Spain, Rodrygo's season-ending ACL tear for Brazil, and Estêvão's hamstring issue further depleting Brazil's attack. Recent ESPN power rankings place Spain atop with unmatched midfield depth via Rodri and Pedri, while France boasts versatile forwards; England, Brazil, and defending champions Argentina trail closely on squad pedigree and favorable group draws avoiding early clashes among top seeds. The expanded 48-team format advances 32 squads, amplifying knockout volatility and keeping the race competitive as fitness uncertainties loom just weeks from kickoff.

markets.question: OGC Nice vs. FC Metz: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Ligue 1 game between OGC Nice and FC Metz, scheduled for May 17 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if OGC Nice and FC Metz combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligue1.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: OGC Nice vs. FC Metz - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Ligue 1 game, scheduled for May 17 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: OGC Nice, sitting 15th in Ligue 1 with 31 points after a 1-1 home draw against Lens in matchweek 32, face a critical home clash against relegated bottom-side FC Metz on May 17 at Allianz Riviera. Desperate to secure survival and avoid the relegation playoff—especially ahead of their pivotal away trip to 16th-placed Auxerre—the hosts hold a strong historical edge, unbeaten in recent home fixtures versus Metz and boasting just two losses in 19 such encounters. However, injuries plague Nice, with forward Elye Wahi (thigh), defender Moïse Bombito (lower leg), and others sidelined, mirroring Metz's extensive absences including Habib Diallo (hamstring) and multiple defenders. Metz's poor away form and demotivation post-relegation could favor a low-scoring affair, though Nice's leaky defense (58 goals conceded) adds upset risk.

markets.question: Will there be at least 1900 measles cases in the U.S. by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there have been the specified amount or more confirmed cases of Measles (Rubeola) in humans in the territory of the United States of America in 2026, according to the CDC case counter by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter 'Total Cases' in 2026 (see: https://www.cdc.gov/measles/data-research/index.html) at the resolution time. If the counter becomes unavailable, another credible source will be used.

Note: Only cases reported by the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter will qualify, regardless of reports from U.S. State agencies or other sources.

events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there have been the specified amount or more confirmed cases of Measles (Rubeola) in humans in the territory of the United States of America in 2026, according to the CDC case counter by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Note: Only cases reported by the CDC Measles (Rubeola) counter will qualify, regardless of reports from U.S. State agencies or other sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: As of May 7, 2026, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) reports 1,842 confirmed measles cases in the United States year-to-date, spanning 39 jurisdictions with 93% outbreak-associated across 25 new 2026 outbreaks and a 6% hospitalization rate but no fatalities. Weekly cases by rash onset have dropped sharply to seven for the May 3 week—down from March peaks over 100—signaling containment amid sustained low kindergarten MMR vaccination coverage of 92.5%, below the 95% herd immunity threshold needed to curb this highly contagious virus (R0 12–18). Nearly all cases (92%) involve unvaccinated or unknown-status individuals, primarily from international importations amid global surges. Traders should monitor CDC weekly surveillance updates through May 31 for potential travel-linked flares or vaccination campaign impacts.
